basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Al-Ṣāffāt opens with a solemn oath by the angels ranged in tight ranks for worship, those who drive back and those who chant the remembrance — a cosmic figure of ordered obedience — to proclaim the object of the oath: “Your God, in truth, is One.” The sūra unfolds the transcendence of the Lord of the heavens, the earth and the sunrises, then confronts the denial of resurrection and paints the Day of Decision when the wrongdoers, gathered with their likes, hurl reproaches at one another. Against them shines the luminous exception, refrain of the whole sūra: “save the servants devoted to God” (al-mukhlaṣīn). Qushayrī makes it the spiritual heart of the text: ikhlāṣ is to consecrate servanthood to the Real alone, to purify the act of all regard for creatures, and to view oneself with the eye of deficiency. Then comes the felicity of the elect in the Gardens, their cups that gladden without intoxicating, and their recollection of former companions glimpsed in the midst of the Blaze. Then files past the gallery of the prophets — Noah answered, Abraham of the sound heart commanded to sacrifice his son and ransomed by a mighty victim, Isaac, Moses and Aaron, Elijah, Lot, Jonah saved from the fish's belly by his glorification — each sealed with the same refrain: “Thus do We reward those who do good.” Qushayrī reads in it the law of the trial: the trial is a trial only so long as its outcome stays hidden; and for the lover, the very tribulation becomes sweetness. The sūra culminates in the transcendence of God above what they ascribe, and is sealed on peace upon the messengers and praise to the Lord of the worlds.
themes: the oath by the ranged angels
the trial of Abraham and the mighty ransom

versets.trad: By those (the angels) ranged in ranks,
versets.commentaire: God opens the sūra with an oath by the angels ranged in the heaven, in the air, and in their stations, never leaving the post the Real has assigned them. Qushayrī: they embrace His command, glorify and sanctify Him, and obey Him in all He bids. The tight rank is the very image of the soul aligned upon the divine order.

versets.trad: those who drive back with vigour,
versets.commentaire: Linked to the first by the letter fā, these are the angels who drive the clouds, or who turn people from sins. Qushayrī adds an inner reading: they are the inner promptings (al-khawāṭir) that rebuke and ward the soul away from the forbidden.

versets.trad: those who recite the remembrance!
versets.commentaire: The angels who recite God's Book and convey revelation to the prophets. Qushayrī also evokes the birds ranged in the sky as an echo of this cosmic chanting. All creation, in its ranks, is a choir of remembrance.

versets.trad: Your God, in truth, is One;
versets.commentaire: Here is the object of the oath. They marvelled that One alone could govern all the world's states. Qushayrī clarifies the meaning of the One: His oneness is that He transcends division in His right, is holy beyond all likeness in His being, and is free of partner in His dominion — one in His majesty, one in His acts, one in His grandeur.

versets.ar: رَبُّ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا وَرَبُّ الْمَشَارِقِ
versets.tr: rabbu s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-mā baynahumā wa-rabbu l-mashāriqi
versets.trad: the Lord of the heavens and the earth and what is between them, the Lord of the sunrises.
versets.commentaire: Master and creator of the heavens, the earth, and what lies between. Qushayrī opens the “sunrises” to a spiritual sense: the risings of the stars, the sun and the moon — but also the sunrises of hearts, with their suns, moons and stars. The heart too has its dawns of knowledge.

versets.ar: إِنَّا زَيَّنَّا السَّمَاءَ الدُّنْيَا بِزِينَةٍ الْكَوَاكِبِ
versets.tr: innā zayyannā s-samāʾa d-dunyā bi-zīnatin il-kawākibi
versets.trad: We have adorned the lowest heaven with an ornament of stars,
versets.commentaire: God adorned the nearest heaven with stars. Qushayrī unfolds the image: likewise He adorned the hearts of His friends with the stars of knowledge and spiritual states. Firmament and heart receive one same ornament of lights.

versets.trad: and as a guard against every rebellious devil.
versets.commentaire: He made the stars missiles against the devils. Qushayrī: likewise He adorned hearts with the lights of tawḥīd, so that when the devil draws near, he is stoned by the stars of their knowledge. The light of oneness is the heart's guard.

versets.ar: لَّا يَسَّمَّعُونَ إِلَى الْمَلَإِ الْأَعْلَىٰ وَيُقْذَفُونَ مِن كُلِّ جَانِبٍ
versets.tr: lā yassammaʿūna ilā l-malaʾi l-aʿlā wa-yuqdhafūna min kulli jānibin
versets.trad: They cannot eavesdrop on the highest assembly, and are pelted from every side,
versets.commentaire: The devils cannot surprise the deliberations of the highest angelic assembly (al-malaʾ al-aʿlā), the host that confers over the carrying-out of the divine decrees. The secret of Heaven stays sealed to whoever has no share in purity. No intruder reaches the council of light.

mots.racine: م ل أ / ع ل و
mots.def: the host of the nearest angels.

versets.trad: driven off, and theirs is a perpetual torment;
versets.commentaire: Chased away and doomed to a torment that never ceases. Whoever stands outside obedience remains in distance, and distance from the Presence is the torment that never breaks off. Banishment far from the light is itself the chastisement.

versets.ar: إِلَّا مَنْ خَطِفَ الْخَطْفَةَ فَأَتْبَعَهُ شِهَابٌ ثَاقِبٌ
versets.tr: illā man khaṭifa l-khaṭfata fa-atbaʿahu shihābun thāqibun
versets.trad: except him who snatches a fragment, whereupon a piercing flame pursues him.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads the “flame” as a parable of the heart: likewise, when the devil seizes the chance to cast some whispering at God's friends, they take heed through remembrance — and at once they see clearly and return to God. He cites: “those who are mindful, when a touch from the devil afflicts them, remember.”

mots.racine: ش ه ب / ث ق ب

versets.ar: فَاسْتَفْتِهِمْ أَهُمْ أَشَدُّ خَلْقًا أَم مَّنْ خَلَقْنَا ۚ إِنَّا خَلَقْنَاهُم مِّن طِينٍ لَّازِبٍ
versets.tr: fa-staftihim a-hum ashaddu khalqan am man khalaqnā innā khalaqnāhum min ṭīnin lāzibin
versets.trad: Ask them whether they are stronger in creation than the other beings We have created. Indeed, We created them from sticky clay.
versets.commentaire: God confronts them with their own impotence. Qushayrī: He made them know their incapacity to establish anything, their weakness in every state, then reminded them of their origin: clinging clay. To recall whence one came shatters the pride of one who thinks himself indestructible.

mots.racine: ش د د / خ ل ق
ط ي ن / ل ز ب

versets.trad: Rather, you marvel, while they mock.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the reality of wonder is the soul's alteration before that whose like custom has not accustomed it to occur. One also reads “ʿajibtu” (I marvel), which Qushayrī admits as an expression of the matter's gravity, in blame or in praise. The prophet marvels at the Real; they jeer in blindness.

versets.trad: And when they are reminded, they take no heed,
versets.commentaire: When admonished by God's signs, they turn from faith and reflection. The reminder profits only the heart disposed to receive; the sealed heart hears the words yet stays deaf to the meaning.

versets.trad: and when they see a sign, they seek to deride it,
versets.commentaire: Instead of bowing before the sign, they egg one another on to mockery. The sign asks for humility; derision is the flight of one who refuses to be touched.

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا إِنْ هَٰذَا إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: wa-qālū in hādhā illā siḥrun mubīnun
versets.trad: saying: “This is nothing but manifest magic!
versets.commentaire: They say: what Muḥammad brings is but manifest magic. Lacking knowledge, they call magic what their heart cannot contain; denial dresses impotence in the name of the obvious.

mots.racine: س ح ر / ب ي ن

versets.ar: أَإِذَا مِتْنَا وَكُنَّا تُرَابًا وَعِظَامًا أَإِنَّا لَمَبْعُوثُونَ
versets.tr: a-idhā mitnā wa-kunnā turāban wa-ʿiẓāman a-innā la-mabʿūthūna
versets.trad: When we are dead and become dust and bones, shall we then be raised,
versets.commentaire: They object: once our parts are scattered and turned to dust, shall we be raised? Qushayrī: they said it in disbelief — knowledge being lost to them, their sight shut, and their hearts barred from tawḥīd.

versets.trad: we and our forefathers of old?”
versets.commentaire: They marvel too that their most ancient ancestors could be brought back to life. Disbelief takes root when one measures God's power by the yardstick of one's own weakness.

versets.trad: Say: “Yes, and you will be abased!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Say to them, O Muḥammad: yes, and in the lowliest state He will raise you, with a single cry He will gather you, after establishing the Resurrection over you all. Denial does not halt the decree; it adds only abasement.

versets.ar: فَإِنَّمَا هِيَ زَجْرَةٌ وَاحِدَةٌ فَإِذَا هُمْ يَنظُرُونَ
versets.tr: fa-innamā hiya zajratun wāḥidatun fa-idhā hum yanẓurūna
versets.trad: It will be but a single cry, and behold, they will be looking on,
versets.commentaire: A single call suffices: there they are, raised and staring, dumbfounded. What seemed impossible to them happens in an instant; the power that creates from clay does not labour to recall the dust.

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا يَا وَيْلَنَا هَٰذَا يَوْمُ الدِّينِ
versets.tr: wa-qālū yā waylanā hādhā yawmu d-dīni
versets.trad: and they will cry: “Woe to us! This is the Day of Judgement!”
versets.commentaire: They call woe upon themselves. What they denied in mockery they now behold with their own eyes; denial turns to lament when the truth uncovers itself.

versets.ar: هَٰذَا يَوْمُ الْفَصْلِ الَّذِي كُنتُم بِهِ تُكَذِّبُونَ
versets.tr: hādhā yawmu l-faṣli lladhī kuntum bihi tukadhdhibūna
versets.trad: This is the Day of Decision that you used to deny!
versets.commentaire: They are told: this is the Day of Sorting you denied — now you see it before you. The edge of this Day separates the true from the false; no denial stands before the unveiled evidence.

versets.ar: احْشُرُوا الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا وَأَزْوَاجَهُمْ وَمَا كَانُوا يَعْبُدُونَ
versets.tr: uḥshurū lladhīna ẓalamū wa-azwājahum wa-mā kānū yaʿbudūna
versets.trad: Gather those who did wrong, together with their consorts and what they used to worship
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads “their consorts” as their companions and likes, those who acted as they did, and whoever aided them in their wrongdoing, little or much. And he draws a lesson for the Path: whoever helps a man in his lapse or his stumble becomes his partner in the punishment and the banishment.

versets.ar: مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ فَاهْدُوهُمْ إِلَىٰ صِرَاطِ الْجَحِيمِ
versets.tr: min dūni llāhi fa-hdūhum ilā ṣirāṭi l-jaḥīmi
versets.trad: besides God, then lead them to the path of the Blaze,
versets.commentaire: They are “guided” — a mockery of the word — toward the road of Hell. They followed false deities instead of God; the way they chose for themselves now leads them where it leads.

mots.racine: ص ر ط / ج ح م

versets.trad: and halt them! They are to be questioned:
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the station of questioning is a fearsome station. He distinguishes those whom the angels question from those whom the King questions Himself; and among them the elect, whom the Real veils from creatures' sight here and hereafter, and the people of lapses, whom God pities without disgracing. The question unveils, but mercy covers His own.

versets.trad: “Why do you not help one another?”
versets.commentaire: They are taunted: where are your supports? Those who aided one another in error find themselves powerless on the Day when none helps any; the alliances of iniquity collapse at the hour of reckoning.

versets.trad: Indeed, that Day they will surrender;
versets.commentaire: That Day they yield, powerless. The submission they refused out of pride is wrung from them by force; but to submit too late is no longer to submit in love.

versets.trad: and they will turn to one another, questioning.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they cast the blame upon one another; this one disowns his fellow, and the fellow disowns him, until God strikes them all with disgrace and unites them in the curse.

versets.ar: قَالُوا إِنَّكُمْ كُنتُمْ تَأْتُونَنَا عَنِ الْيَمِينِ
versets.tr: qālū innakum kuntum taʾtūnanā ʿani l-yamīni
versets.trad: They will say: “You used to come at us from the right!”
versets.commentaire: The followers accuse their leaders of approaching them from the side of rectitude — under cover of good — to turn them aside. The most dangerous evil presents itself from the side of good; it disguises straying as straightness.

versets.trad: They will reply: “Rather, you were not believers;
versets.commentaire: The leaders retort: you had no faith from the outset. Each flings the weight of his loss upon the other; but the heart without faith would have let no one turn it aside.

versets.ar: وَمَا كَانَ لَنَا عَلَيْكُم مِّن سُلْطَانٍ ۖ بَلْ كُنتُمْ قَوْمًا طَاغِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mā kāna lanā ʿalaykum min sulṭānin bal kuntum qawman ṭāghīna
versets.trad: we had no authority over you, but you yourselves were a transgressing people.
versets.commentaire: The leaders: we had over you no compelling power; you were transgressors yourselves. Seduction only reveals the inclination already there; none is dragged off who does not consent to be.

versets.ar: فَحَقَّ عَلَيْنَا قَوْلُ رَبِّنَا ۖ إِنَّا لَذَائِقُونَ
versets.tr: fa-ḥaqqa ʿalaynā qawlu rabbinā innā la-dhāʾiqūna
versets.trad: The word of our Lord has come true against us, and we are going to taste it.
versets.commentaire: God's decree has proved true against them; they will taste its fruit. When the Lord's word falls, there is no refuge; to acknowledge too late does not spare the taste of the chastisement.

versets.trad: We led you astray, for we ourselves were astray.”
versets.commentaire: The leaders confess: we led you astray because we were astray. The blind leading the blind hurl both into the pit; shared straying only deepens the common fall.

versets.trad: That Day, they will all share in the chastisement;
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they share in the chastisement, but their portions differ — just as they shared in the fault, their faults were of unequal measure. Justice weighs each according to his share.

versets.trad: thus do We deal with the guilty.
versets.commentaire: Such is God's way with the criminals. The law of requital is constant: whoever sows crime reaps its just measure.

versets.ar: إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا إِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ
versets.tr: innahum kānū idhā qīla lahum lā ilāha illā llāhu yastakbirūna
versets.trad: When it was said to them, “There is no god but God,” they grew arrogant
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: veiling themselves by their own hearts cast them into the abyss of their chastisement, for they disdained to acknowledge His lordship. Had they known Him, they would have gloried in serving Him. He cites: “those who are near your Lord do not disdain to worship Him.” Whoever knows God finds joy only in His service.

versets.ar: وَيَقُولُونَ أَئِنَّا لَتَارِكُو آلِهَتِنَا لِشَاعِرٍ مَّجْنُونٍ
versets.tr: wa-yaqūlūna a-innā la-tārikū ālihatinā li-shāʿirin majnūnin
versets.trad: and said: “Are we to forsake our gods for a possessed poet?”
versets.commentaire: They refuse to leave their idols for a “possessed poet.” Qushayrī observes: having no shame to ascribe to the Most High what ill-befits His majesty, they did not hesitate to load His prophets with vices. Whoever blasphemes against God readily slanders His messengers.

mots.racine: ش ع ر / ج ن ن

versets.trad: Rather, he came with the Truth and confirmed the messengers.
versets.commentaire: Far from being mad, he came with the Truth and corroborated all the messengers. True speech does not contradict true speech; the seal of the prophets confirms the whole chain of revelation.

versets.trad: Indeed, you will taste the painful chastisement.
versets.commentaire: They are warned: you will taste the searing torment. Denial has its term; what one has mocked, one ends by tasting.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / أ ل م

versets.ar: وَمَا تُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا مَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā tujzawna illā mā kuntum taʿmalūna
versets.trad: You will be requited only for what you used to do.
versets.commentaire: No injustice: the requital matches the deed. The Real wrongs no one; each reaps exactly what he sowed.

versets.trad: But the sincere servants of God,
versets.commentaire: Here is the luminous exception to the chastisement: the servants devoted to God alone. Qushayrī heaps up definitions of ikhlāṣ: sincerity is to consecrate servanthood to the Real alone; one whose work is mixed with display is not sincere. It is to purify the act of all regard for creatures. It is to see persons no more. It is to view oneself with the eye of deficiency.

versets.trad: theirs is a known provision:
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they have a provision fixed at appointed times. For the people of Paradise, in the hereafter, a known provision for their bodies and their inmost secrets: the rich have the portion of their souls, the poor that of their hearts and secrets. The provender of the elect feeds outer and inmost alike.

mots.racine: ر ز ق / ع ل م

versets.trad: fruits shall be offered them, and they shall be honoured,
versets.commentaire: Fruits, and honour. Qushayrī adds a fine touch: among these honours is the coming of God's messenger to them at every moment — and likewise, today, the divine address descends at every moment upon the hearts of the elect. The greatest fruit is the address of the Beloved.

versets.trad: set in the Gardens of Delight,
versets.commentaire: In the Gardens of bliss. The promised delight is not merely sensual enjoyment, but the soul's repose in the nearness of its Lord.

mots.racine: ج ن ن / ن ع م

versets.trad: upon couches, facing one another.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they find comfort in seeing one another, and ease in meeting. The face-to-face of the blessed is the image of the concord of purified hearts.

versets.trad: A cup from a flowing spring will be passed around them,
versets.commentaire: A cup drawn from a living spring is passed among them. Qushayrī: a drink that brings them joy with no anxiety there, a drink that makes them present without intoxicating them. The drink of Paradise exalts without robbing the reason.

versets.trad: clear and delicious to the drinkers,
versets.commentaire: White, delectable to the drinker. The purity of the colour figures the limpidity of this joy: a pleasure with no trouble or dregs.

versets.ar: لَا فِيهَا غَوْلٌ وَلَا هُمْ عَنْهَا يُنزَفُونَ
versets.tr: lā fīhā ghawlun wa-lā hum ʿanhā yunzafūna
versets.trad: causing them neither intoxication nor stupor.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it does not rob their minds, nor strip their dignity, nor lift their reverence from them. Some drink under the veil, others are given to drink in the Presence, in the character of nearness. The heavenly wine increases lucidity instead of effacing it.

versets.trad: And with them will be maidens of restrained glance, wide-eyed,
versets.commentaire: Companions of restrained glance, wide-eyed. Qushayrī: they look only at their spouse. The restrained gaze is the emblem of a love wholly turned toward the one beloved.

mots.racine: ق ص ر / ط ر ف

versets.trad: as if they were hidden pearls.
versets.commentaire: Likened to preserved pearls. What is kept hidden stays intact; purity guarded away from the gaze is the most precious.

mots.racine: ب ي ض / ك ن ن

versets.trad: They will turn to one another, questioning.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they converse among themselves, recalling among their acquaintances those who did not believe in God. At the height of bliss they remember the faces of old — and God will grant them to behold its outcome.

versets.ar: قَالَ قَائِلٌ مِّنْهُمْ إِنِّي كَانَ لِي قَرِينٌ
versets.tr: qāla qāʾilun minhum innī kāna lī qarīnun
versets.trad: A speaker among them will say: “I had a companion
versets.commentaire: One of the blessed recalls a former companion who stayed unbelieving. The memory of Paradise does not efface the recollection of souls met here below; rather it measures the contrast of destinies.

versets.trad: who used to say: ‘Are you of those who affirm the truth?
versets.commentaire: The companion mocked him over his faith: do you really believe? The scoffer of old held faith improbable; but it is the believer who, in the end, stands in the light.

versets.ar: أَإِذَا مِتْنَا وَكُنَّا تُرَابًا وَعِظَامًا أَإِنَّا لَمَدِينُونَ
versets.tr: a-idhā mitnā wa-kunnā turāban wa-ʿiẓāman a-innā la-madīnūna
versets.trad: When we are dead and become dust and bones, shall we truly be judged?’”
versets.commentaire: The companion denied the judgement after death. Doubt about requital is the root of all laxity; to deny the reckoning is to think the act without consequence.

versets.trad: He will add: “Will you look down?”
versets.commentaire: The blessed one invites his companions to look below. God creates in them the faculty to behold the fate of the unbeliever who burns; the contrast strengthens the gratitude of the saved.

versets.trad: Then he will look and see him in the midst of the Blaze.
versets.commentaire: He leans and sees his former companion in the midst of the fire. Yesterday's scoffer lies at the heart of the flame; what one denies does not therefore vanish — it waits at the end.

mots.racine: س و ي / ج ح م

versets.trad: He will say: “By God! You nearly ruined me.
versets.commentaire: The saved one cries: by God, you almost brought my ruin! To consort with the denier is a peril to faith; the companion in straying nearly drags one to the abyss.

versets.ar: وَلَوْلَا نِعْمَةُ رَبِّي لَكُنتُ مِنَ الْمُحْضَرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lawlā niʿmatu rabbī la-kuntu mina l-muḥḍarīna
versets.trad: Had it not been for the grace of my Lord, I would have been among those arraigned.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes a subtle nuance: the friend spoke true, but without attaining pure tawḥīd, for he placed “grace” as an intermediary; it would have been more perfect to say: “were it not for my Lord, I would have been arraigned.” He spoke from the eye of separation, not the eye of union.

mots.racine: ن ع م / ر ب ب

versets.trad: Are we not to die
versets.commentaire: The blessed one marvels: are we no longer subject to death? True felicity is the eternity of nearness; where there is no more death, there is no more separation.

versets.ar: إِلَّا مَوْتَتَنَا الْأُولَىٰ وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمُعَذَّبِينَ
versets.tr: illā mawtatanā l-ūlā wa-mā naḥnu bi-muʿadhdhabīna
versets.trad: save our first death, and shall we not be chastised?”
versets.commentaire: Save the first death, no more dying nor torment. The single passage of earthly death is crossed; beyond remains only life without end, free of chastisement.

mots.racine: م و ت / أ و ل

versets.trad: This, indeed, is the supreme triumph!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: perhaps it is the angels who say this to them; or the Real, when He shows them their place in Paradise. The supreme triumph is not only to escape the fire, but to attain the station of contemplation.

mots.racine: ف و ز / ع ظ م

versets.trad: For the like of this let the workers work!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī kindles: when a gleam of the realities shines, a dawn of union, or an atom of the breath of nearness, it is fitting indeed that men cry out: for such a state souls give themselves! And he concludes: here expressions run dry and allusions fail.

versets.ar: أَذَٰلِكَ خَيْرٌ نُّزُلًا أَمْ شَجَرَةُ الزَّقُّومِ
versets.tr: a-dhālika khayrun nuzulan am shajaratu z-zaqqūmi
versets.trad: Is that not a better welcome than the tree of Zaqqūm?
versets.commentaire: God contrasts the welcome of the elect with that of the enemies. Qushayrī: He described the abasement of the enemies, their state of degradation and torment in the fire, the tree of Zaqqūm hideous as the heads of devils — then their return to the Blaze.

versets.trad: We have made it a trial for the wrongdoers;
versets.commentaire: The tree is a trial for the unjust. What scandalizes the unbeliever — a tree growing in the fire — becomes for him a cause of doubt; but the trial reveals who bows and who rebels.

versets.ar: إِنَّهَا شَجَرَةٌ تَخْرُجُ فِي أَصْلِ الْجَحِيمِ
versets.tr: innahā shajaratun takhruju fī aṣli l-jaḥīmi
versets.trad: it is a tree that grows in the depths of the Blaze;
versets.commentaire: A tree that springs from the depths of Hell. What the fire does not consume is the bitterness of the chastisement itself — a tree that feeds on fire to feed the torment.

mots.racine: أ ص ل / ج ح م

versets.trad: its fruit is like the heads of devils.
versets.commentaire: Its fruits resemble the heads of devils. The hideousness of the image tells the nature of the forbidden fruit: what is born of rebellion bears the face of evil.

mots.racine: ر أ س / ش ط ن

versets.ar: فَإِنَّهُمْ لَآكِلُونَ مِنْهَا فَمَالِئُونَ مِنْهَا الْبُطُونَ
versets.tr: fa-innahum la-ākilūna minhā fa-māliʾūna minhā l-buṭūna
versets.trad: The guilty will eat of it and fill their bellies with it;
versets.commentaire: They will eat of it until they gorge their bellies. The greed that drove them on earth pursues them in the torment: they fill themselves with bitterness as they had filled themselves with illusions.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ إِنَّ لَهُمْ عَلَيْهَا لَشَوْبًا مِّنْ حَمِيمٍ
versets.tr: thumma inna lahum ʿalayhā la-shawban min ḥamīmin
versets.trad: then atop it they will drink a scalding brew,
versets.commentaire: On top of this, a drink of boiling water. To the fire of the food is added the fire of the drink; the chastisement leaves no respite to one who sought no refuge.

versets.trad: then they will return to the Blaze.
versets.commentaire: Their return, in the end, is to the fire. Every escape they think to seize brings them back to the fire; the term of straying is the return to what one has chosen.

versets.trad: They found their fathers astray,
versets.commentaire: They inherited the straying of their fathers. To follow forebears without examination is the snare of conformism; tradition transmitted in error perpetuates error.

versets.trad: and they hastened upon their footsteps.
versets.commentaire: They run upon their fathers' tracks without thinking. Eager imitation without discernment is the mark of the soul that flees the labour of knowing.

versets.trad: Most of the ancients before them went astray;
versets.commentaire: Before them, most of the first peoples went astray. Number is no guarantor of truth; the majority in error remains error, ancient though it be.

mots.racine: ك ث ر / أ و ل
mots.def: the great number of the first.

versets.trad: yet We had sent among them warners.
versets.commentaire: God did not leave them without warners. The proof was set up against them; their loss comes not from a lack of call but from a refusal to hear.

versets.trad: So consider what was the end of those who had been warned,
versets.commentaire: Look at the end of those who had been warned: ruin. Meditation on the fate of past nations is a mirror held up to the present.

versets.trad: save the sincere servants of God.
versets.commentaire: Only the servants devoted to God alone escape the dire fate. Ikhlāṣ — the sincerity that purifies the act of every alien regard — is the ark that saves from the common shipwreck. This exception, repeated for each prophet, scans the sūra as a refrain of salvation.

versets.trad: Noah called upon Us, and how excellent are We who answer!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sings the reciprocity of the call: when his people's harm reached him, he returned to Us; he called Us and We called him, he spoke to Us and We spoke to him — how excellent a responder He was for us, and how excellent responders We were for him!

versets.trad: We saved him and his family from the terrible distress,
versets.commentaire: God saved him and his family from the great calamity. Qushayrī, elegantly: how vast the distance between Noah's distress and his son's! Salvation spares the near of heart, not the near of blood.

mots.racine: ك ر ب / ع ظ م

versets.trad: and We made his descendants the survivors,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: for all mankind descends from the children of Noah — those with him in the ark having left no posterity. All humanity, after the flood, proceeds from the line of the saved.

versets.trad: and We left for him among later generations:
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: this denotes what people will say of him until the Day of Resurrection. The good name is a praise left as inheritance across the ages.

versets.trad: “Peace be upon Noah throughout the worlds!”
versets.commentaire: Peace upon Noah among all creatures. The divine greeting is the seal of acceptance; to be named in peace throughout the worlds is to enter the blessed memory of the universe.

versets.trad: Thus do We reward those who do good.
versets.commentaire: Refrain of the sūra: such is the requital of the excellent. Iḥsān — to worship God as if seeing Him — calls forth the fairest reward: a good name here below and peace in the hereafter.

mots.def: those who do good with excellence.

versets.trad: He was one of Our believing servants.
versets.commentaire: He was one of Our believing servants. The root of all excellence is faith; the good name and salvation flow from servanthood lived in belief.

versets.trad: Then We drowned the others.
versets.commentaire: Then God drowned the others. The same flood that bears the believer's ark engulfs the denier; the water of salvation and the water of chastisement are one water, set apart by faith.

versets.trad: Abraham was indeed of his party.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Abraham is of Noah's party in tawḥīd — even though their legal branches differ. The prophets form one spiritual family, united by oneness beyond the diversity of laws.

versets.trad: When he came to his Lord with a sound heart;
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the “sound heart” as a sheaf of meanings: without any flaw; stung with love; safe from the love of other-than-Him; free of the portions of its soul and its own will; surrendered to God in His decree and His choice. The pure heart is the one that shares nothing with God.

mots.racine: ق ل ب / س ل م
mots.def: upright heart, free of all vice.

versets.ar: إِذْ قَالَ لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوْمِهِ مَاذَا تَعْبُدُونَ
versets.tr: idh qāla li-abīhi wa-qawmihi mādhā taʿbudūna
versets.trad: when he said to his father and his people: “What do you worship?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he questioned them to blame them and point out the place of their error. Abraham's question awaits no answer: it rouses the conscience asleep before the absurd.

versets.trad: Is it falsehood — gods besides God — that you desire?
versets.commentaire: Do you, in imposture, want gods besides God? Idolatry is first an inner lie: one gives one's desire to what is nothing, turning the heart from the sole Reality.

versets.trad: What then is your thought of the Lord of the worlds?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī conveys the question's reach: when you meet Him, having worshipped another — what will you say to Him? How will you be, in the station of shame, before what will be there, you who today neglect Him? Abraham's question casts the heart forward to the hour of the face-to-face.

mots.racine: ر ب ب / ع ل م

versets.trad: Then he gazed intently into the stars
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, following a fine gloss, distinguishes “gazing into” from “gazing toward”: “to gaze into the stars” means he meditated and reflected, whereas “to gaze toward” would be but a glance — there is a difference between contemplation by thought and insight, and the mere look of the eye.

versets.trad: and said: “I am indeed ailing!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reports that fever would seize him at a known hour, and that he made use of this to avoid following his people to their feast. It is said this was one of those double-meaning utterances (maʿārīḍ) — he meant what was true, in order secretly to carry out the design of breaking the idols. Wisdom knows how to speak the truth obliquely.

versets.trad: whereupon they turned their backs on him.
versets.commentaire: They went off, turning their backs on him. God grants His friend the solitude needed for his act; the withdrawal of others is sometimes the moment offered to truth to act.

versets.ar: فَرَاغَ إِلَىٰ آلِهَتِهِمْ فَقَالَ أَلَا تَأْكُلُونَ
versets.tr: fa-rāgha ilā ālihatihim fa-qāla a-lā taʾkulūna
versets.trad: Then he stole to their gods and said: “What, do you not eat?
versets.commentaire: He slipped over to the idols and questioned them, ironic: do you not eat? Abraham's irony unmasks the nothingness of the idols; he treats them as beings the better to reveal that they are nothing.

versets.trad: Why do you not speak?”
versets.commentaire: Why do you say nothing? The muteness of the idol is the proof of its impotence; what neither speaks nor hears merits neither worship nor fear.

versets.trad: Then he turned upon them, striking with his right hand.
versets.commentaire: He turned upon them and struck with his right. Abraham's act breaks not only the stone but the illusion: he shatters what custom had made sacred.

mots.def: with the right hand, with force.

versets.trad: The people came rushing toward him.
versets.commentaire: They came back to him in haste. The truth that unmasks the idol unleashes the fury of those who fed on it; but the crowd's haste changes nothing of the unveiled evidence.

versets.trad: He said: “Do you worship what you carve,
versets.commentaire: Do you worship what your own hands have carved? The argument is unanswerable: one cannot serve the work of one's hands; the sculptor is greater than his statue.

versets.trad: when it is God who created you and what you make?”
versets.commentaire: God created you and what you do. The Creator precedes and embraces His creature's act; to worship one's own handiwork is to overturn the order of the real.

versets.ar: قَالُوا ابْنُوا لَهُ بُنْيَانًا فَأَلْقُوهُ فِي الْجَحِيمِ
versets.tr: qālū ibnū lahu bunyānan fa-alqūhu fī l-jaḥīmi
versets.trad: They said: “Build for him a structure and cast him into the blaze.”
versets.commentaire: Lacking argument, they answer with violence: a pyre. When reason is beaten, pride resorts to force; but the fire kindled against the truth will only reveal the divine protection.

versets.trad: They sought to plot against him, but it was they whom We brought utterly low.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God turned their ruse back upon their own throats. He reports that Gabriel presented himself to Abraham, hurled by the catapult into the air: “Do you have any need?” — “Of you, no!” answered the friend, who awaited his help from God alone.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ إِنِّي ذَاهِبٌ إِلَىٰ رَبِّي سَيَهْدِينِ
versets.tr: wa-qāla innī dhāhibun ilā rabbī sa-yahdīni
versets.trad: And he said: “I am going to my Lord; He will guide me.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opens this verse with several pearls. He asked for a particular guidance, being already guided; or a guidance toward himself, for he had lost in God his heart and soul — “He will guide me to myself, that I may fulfil the right of His servanthood,” for one who is absorbed in the realities of union cannot perform worship unless restored to distinction. And he distinguishes: Abraham spoke from the eye of separation, Moses from the eye of union, our Prophet from the eye of the union of union.

versets.trad: My Lord! Grant me one of the righteous.”
versets.commentaire: Abraham implores righteous offspring. The friend's purest desire is not just any posterity, but a line among the righteous; he asks God not for an heir, but for a servant.

versets.trad: So We gave him the good news of a forbearing boy.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes the delicacy of the epithet: by saying “forbearing” (ḥalīm), He intimated that the boy would meet a trial requiring patience to bear it. The name given already foretells the destiny: gentleness will be tested by the sacrifice.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا بَلَغَ مَعَهُ السَّعْيَ قَالَ يَا بُنَيَّ إِنِّي أَرَىٰ فِي الْمَنَامِ أَنِّي أَذْبَحُكَ فَانظُرْ مَاذَا تَرَىٰ ۚ قَالَ يَا أَبَتِ افْعَلْ مَا تُؤْمَرُ ۖ سَتَجِدُنِي إِن شَاءَ اللَّهُ مِنَ الصَّابِرِينَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā balagha maʿahu s-saʿya qāla yā bunayya innī arā fī l-manāmi annī adhbaḥuka fa-nẓur mādhā tarā qāla yā abati fʿal mā tuʾmaru sa-tajidunī in shāʾa llāhu mina ṣ-ṣābirīna
versets.trad: When he reached the age of striving with him, he said: “O my son! I have seen in a dream that I am to sacrifice you; consider, what do you think?” He said: “O my father! Do what you are commanded. If God wills, you will find me among the patient.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this moment the instant the heart binds itself to the child. He ascribes to Ishmael a reply of astonishing depth: “If your heart finds its peace in sacrificing your son for God, my heart finds its peace in being sacrificed by my father for God.” And he notes that the child made no claim upon himself, but bowed with the formula of exception: “if God wills.”

versets.trad: Then, when they had both submitted and he had laid him down upon his brow,
versets.commentaire: The culminating instant: both submit, the one offering, the other offering himself. True surrender (islām) is not merely to obey, but to consent with the heart to what reason cannot embrace; father and son abandon themselves together to the decree.

mots.def: to stretch on the side of the brow.

versets.trad: We called out to him: “O Abraham!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reports that the knife passed over the throat without cutting, and that Abraham marvelled; then came the call. The aim of all this was your submission: it was not the blood that was wanted, but the total surrender of both hearts.

versets.ar: قَدْ صَدَّقْتَ الرُّؤْيَا ۚ إِنَّا كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِي الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: qad ṣaddaqta r-ruʾyā innā kadhālika najzī l-muḥsinīna
versets.trad: You have indeed believed the vision. Thus do We reward those who do good;
versets.commentaire: You gave credence to the vision. Qushayrī: God had veiled from them, during the trial, the knowledge of what was wanted of them, and unveiled it only after the time of tribulation, so that the meaning of the trial would not be nullified. The trial is a trial only so long as its outcome stays hidden.

versets.trad: truly this was the manifest test.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī ranks men before the trial: an unbearable trial, proper to the common folk; and a savoured trial, proper to those who relish their tribulations, as though they would not despair even were they slain. For the lover, the trial itself becomes sweetness.

mots.racine: ب ل و / ب ي ن

versets.trad: And We ransomed him with a mighty sacrifice,
versets.commentaire: God ransomed the child with a mighty victim. Qushayrī relates the tradition: the ram of the ransom had been reared in Paradise forty autumns before. The ransom was prepared from all eternity; the sacrifice of the heart precedes and calls forth the gift of grace.

mots.racine: ذ ب ح / ع ظ م

versets.trad: and We perpetuated his memory among later generations:
versets.commentaire: God left for Abraham a lasting renown among the generations. The trial crossed in surrender becomes eternal praise; the sacrifice consented turns into a blessing transmitted.

versets.trad: “Peace be upon Abraham!”
versets.commentaire: Peace upon Abraham. Peace descends upon one who has given up his dearest treasure; total surrender calls forth the divine greeting.

versets.trad: Thus do We reward those who do good.
versets.commentaire: Refrain: such is the requital of the excellent. Whoever fulfils iḥsān, worshipping God as if he saw Him, receives peace engraved in the memory of the worlds.

versets.trad: He was truly one of Our believing servants.
versets.commentaire: He was one of Our believing servants. Abraham's greatness rests not on his exploits but on his faith; servanthood lived in belief is the source of all elevation.

versets.trad: And We gave him the good news of Isaac, a prophet among the righteous.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: all this came after the trial, and he cites: “with hardship is ease.” The birth of Isaac, announced after the consented sacrifice, illustrates the divine law: the gift follows the surrender, ease succeeds the trial.

versets.ar: وَبَارَكْنَا عَلَيْهِ وَعَلَىٰ إِسْحَاقَ ۚ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِهِمَا مُحْسِنٌ وَظَالِمٌ لِّنَفْسِهِ مُبِينٌ
versets.tr: wa-bāraknā ʿalayhi wa-ʿalā isḥāqa wa-min dhurriyyatihimā muḥsinun wa-ẓālimun li-nafsihi mubīnun
versets.trad: And We blessed him and Isaac. Among their descendants are those who do good and those who plainly wrong themselves.
versets.commentaire: God blessed Abraham and Isaac, yet their line is split between good-doers and wrongdoers. The blessing of the stock does not constrain the choice of the branches; each remains free to embrace the light or to wrong himself.

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ن ف س

versets.trad: We bestowed favour upon Moses and Aaron.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He favoured them with prophethood, with deliverance from Pharaoh and his people, and with victory over them. The divine favour toward the two brothers embraces election, deliverance and triumph.

versets.trad: We delivered them and their people from the terrible distress,
versets.commentaire: God saved them and their people from the great calamity of bondage. The salvation of the prophets extends to their community; the grace granted to the guide overflows onto those who follow him.

versets.trad: We helped them, and it was they who prevailed.
versets.commentaire: We helped them and they prevailed. Whom God aids cannot be vanquished; the prophets' victory is not of their strength but of help from above.

versets.trad: We gave them the perfectly clear Book
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it is the Torah. The clear Book is the light that dispels the darkness of doubt; the gift of the Scripture is the crowning of the prophetic mission.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ب ي ن

versets.trad: and guided them on the straight path.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives the “straight path” a mystical sense: by the renouncing of one's own strength and power, and by the contemplation of the very eye of tawḥīd. The straight path is the one where one leans on God alone.

mots.racine: ص ر ط / ق و م

versets.trad: And We perpetuated their memory among later generations:
themes: the memory of Moses and Aaron
versets.commentaire: God left for them a lasting renown. The faithfulness of servants is engraved in the memory of the ages; the good name is the trace that grace leaves.

versets.trad: “Peace be upon Moses and Aaron!”
themes: the greeting to the two brothers
versets.commentaire: Peace upon Moses and Aaron. The divine greeting joins the two brothers in one blessing; brotherhood in service calls forth the joint peace.

versets.trad: Thus do We reward those who do good.
versets.commentaire: Refrain: such is the requital of the excellent. The repetition of the refrain for each prophet weaves the unity of the sūra: one same law of grace for all the good-doers.

versets.trad: They were both of Our believing servants.
themes: the faith of the two brothers
versets.commentaire: They were both of Our believing servants. Faith is the common ground of all the elect; by it the prophets attain the station of accepted service.

versets.trad: Indeed, Elijah was one of the messengers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes: it is said Elijah is Idris, or another; he was in Syria, the name of their idol was Baʿl, and their city Baalbek; he warned his people who denied him, exhorted them without their believing, and his people perished.

versets.trad: When he said to his people: “Will you not fear God?
versets.commentaire: He calls his people to reverent fear. Taqwā is the prophet's first word: before naming the idol, he awakens in the soul the sense of the Lord from whom it has strayed.

versets.trad: Will you call upon Baʿl and forsake the best of creators:
versets.commentaire: Do you call upon Baʿl and forsake the most excellent of creators? To prefer a mute idol to the Creator of all beauty is the height of blindness; one leaves the source for the reflection.

mots.racine: ح س ن / خ ل ق

versets.trad: God, your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers of old?”
versets.commentaire: God, your Lord and that of your forefathers. The Lord who ruled the first is the same who rules the last; to acknowledge His lordship is to rejoin the eternal line of true worship.

versets.trad: They denied him, so they will be arraigned,
versets.commentaire: They denied him: they will therefore be brought to the chastisement. To deny the warner does not ward off the reckoning; denial only seals the arraignment.

versets.trad: save the sincere servants of God.
versets.commentaire: Only the servants devoted to God are excepted. Ikhlāṣ purifies the work of the regard of creatures; the sincere escapes arraignment because he has lived only for the sole Face of God.

versets.trad: And We perpetuated his memory among later generations:
versets.commentaire: God left for Elijah a lasting renown. The faithful servant, though denied by his own, remains honoured in the memory of God and of the ages.

versets.trad: “Peace be upon Elijah and his folk!”
versets.commentaire: Peace upon Elijah and his own. The greeting extends to those who follow his way; to honour the guide is also to bless the community of his faithful.

versets.trad: Thus do We reward those who do good.
versets.commentaire: Refrain: such is the requital of the excellent. The same grace crowns each good-doer; excellence invariably calls forth peace and renown.

versets.trad: He was one of Our believing servants.
versets.commentaire: He was one of Our believing servants. Faith is the golden thread linking all the prophets; it founds their honour and their salvation.

versets.trad: Indeed, Lot was one of the messengers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī briefly recalls: his story is past, and how God saved his family save his wife who shared in her people's disobedience, so the chastisement fell upon her as upon them. The bond of blood does not save one who joins the rebels.

versets.trad: We saved him and all his family,
versets.commentaire: God saved him and all his household. Salvation enfolds those who stay faithful with the prophet; deliverance follows uprightness, not kinship alone.

versets.trad: except an old woman who stayed behind.
versets.commentaire: Save an old woman left among the laggards — Lot's wife. Nearness to the prophet does not profit one who chooses the camp of the rebels; the heart, not the household, decides the destiny.

versets.trad: Then We destroyed the others.
versets.commentaire: Then God annihilated the others. Chastisement strikes whoever persists in transgression; the destruction of the rebel cities is a warning engraved in history.

versets.trad: You pass by their traces in the morning
versets.commentaire: You pass near their ruins in the morning. The vestiges of the chastised peoples line the roads of the living; whoever passes without meditating repeats the blindness of the vanished.

versets.trad: and by night. Will you not reason?
versets.commentaire: And by night too: will you not reason? The repeated passing near the ruins, day and night, is a constant invitation to reflection; the sleeping reason is the true peril.

versets.trad: Indeed, Jonah was one of the messengers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: at first he sought to be excused from prophethood, but he was not excused; then there befell him what befell him. Jonah is the figure of the servant who learns, through trial, to embrace his charge.

versets.trad: He fled to the laden ship,
versets.commentaire: He fled, like a runaway slave, to the crowded ship. Jonah's flight is the movement of the soul that would escape its mission; but none flees the decree of God.

mots.racine: ف ل ك / ش ح ن

versets.trad: then they cast lots, and he was among the losers.
versets.commentaire: Lots were cast and he was chosen. The lot that singles him out is but a veil: it is the divine decree fulfilling itself, leading the servant toward the trial that will purify him.

mots.def: to take part in the drawing.

versets.trad: The fish swallowed him, while he was blameworthy,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the fish swallowed him, while he was blameworthy for what was charged to him; and the Real is free of injustice in His decree, for creation is His. The fish's belly becomes the place where the servant confronts his own failing.

versets.trad: and had he not been of those who glorify
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God observed the right of his worship and preserved the covenant of what he had once rendered Him. Faithfulness to the covenant is, in us, a part of faith, and on His part a part of iḥsān: “the believer has received from God a beautiful character.” The tasbīḥ of old becomes the ark of salvation.

versets.ar: لَلَبِثَ فِي بَطْنِهِ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ يُبْعَثُونَ
versets.tr: la-labitha fī baṭnihi ilā yawmi yubʿathūna
versets.trad: he would have remained in its belly until the Day of Resurrection.
versets.commentaire: Without his glorification, he would have stayed there until the Resurrection. The remembrance of God, sown in the days of ease, becomes on the day of trial the rope that draws the servant out of the dark.

versets.trad: We cast him, spent, upon the barren shore
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “ailing” — weakened by the time spent in the fish's belly. Cast upon the bare strand, Jonah is in the state of a newborn: stripped, frail, restored to pure dependence on God.

versets.trad: and made a gourd plant grow over him.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God made the gourd grow to shade him, for the desert sun was wounding him; and He raised up for him a nursing gazelle whose milk he suckled, as though the Real were returning him to the state of infancy. The divine tenderness surrounds the tried servant with motherly care.

versets.ar: وَأَرْسَلْنَاهُ إِلَىٰ مِائَةِ أَلْفٍ أَوْ يَزِيدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-arsalnāhu ilā miʾati alfin aw yazīdūna
versets.trad: Then We sent him to a hundred thousand or more.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī relates the beautiful lesson given to Jonah, grieved for a potter whose jars would be broken: “O Jonah, your heart softens for a potter who loses a year's work, and you would have Me destroy a hundred thousand of My servants? O Jonah, you did not create them; had you created them, you would have pitied them.” God's mercy infinitely surpasses the servant's.

versets.trad: They believed, and We granted them enjoyment for a while.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī recalls that this people repented on seeing the chastisement dawn, and that God turned it away from them. Sincere repentance, even late, suspends the decree of chastisement; the door of return stays open to whoever turns to God.

versets.trad: Ask them whether your Lord has daughters while they have sons.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī exposes the incoherence: when they claimed the angels are God's daughters, He unveiled the ugliness of their saying — they who scorned daughters and preferred sons, and who, in their unbelief, ascribed to the Eternal what they were ashamed of for themselves!

versets.ar: أَمْ خَلَقْنَا الْمَلَائِكَةَ إِنَاثًا وَهُمْ شَاهِدُونَ
versets.tr: am khalaqnā l-malāʾikata ināthan wa-hum shāhidūna
versets.trad: Or did We create the angels female while they were witnesses?
versets.commentaire: Were they witnesses to the creation of the angels, to call it female? They assert about the unseen what they could not have seen; the claim about the mystery without knowledge is the mark of the impostor.

versets.trad: Surely, out of their imposture they say:
versets.commentaire: Behold, out of sheer imposture they utter a monstrous lie. The lie about God is the gravest of fabrications; it ascribes to the Absolute the failings of the creature.

versets.trad: “God has begotten!” Indeed, they are liars.
versets.commentaire: “God has begotten” — and they are liars. Begetting implies need and likeness; yet God is free of all that. To ascribe to Him offspring is to lower Him to the rank of the created.

versets.trad: Has He preferred daughters to sons?
versets.commentaire: Would He have chosen daughters over sons — by their own scale? They project upon God their petty preferences; but He who transcends gender will not be confined within their categories.

versets.trad: What is the matter with you? How do you judge!
versets.commentaire: What ails you, that you judge thus? The judgement passed upon the divine mystery from one's own failings is an excess; the soul arrogates a verdict over what infinitely surpasses it.

versets.trad: Will you not take heed?
versets.commentaire: Will you not remember? Remembrance (tadhakkur) is the soul's return to the first evidence it has covered over; to meditate is to let the buried knowledge rise again within.

versets.trad: Or do you have manifest authority?
versets.commentaire: Do you have a clear proof? Every assertion about God demands a foundation; without revelation or argument, the claim is but wind.

mots.racine: س ل ط / ب ي ن

versets.trad: Then bring your scripture, if you are truthful!
versets.commentaire: Bring your Book, if you speak true! The challenge is final: no scripture supports their saying; truth proves itself, the lie can produce no title.

versets.ar: وَجَعَلُوا بَيْنَهُ وَبَيْنَ الْجِنَّةِ نَسَبًا ۚ وَلَقَدْ عَلِمَتِ الْجِنَّةُ إِنَّهُمْ لَمُحْضَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-jaʿalū baynahu wa-bayna l-jinnati nasaban wa-laqad ʿalimati l-jinnatu innahum la-muḥḍarūna
versets.trad: They have invented a kinship between Him and the jinn, but the jinn know they will be arraigned...
versets.commentaire: They invent a kinship between God and the jinn. Yet the jinn themselves know they will be arraigned for the reckoning; how could a being destined for judgement share the nature of the One who judges?

versets.trad: Glory be to God, far above what they ascribe!
versets.commentaire: Glory be to God, transcending all they describe. Tanzīh — the declaration of the divine purity — washes the Name of every unworthy ascription; to say “subḥān Allāh” is to render God to His infinite purity.

versets.trad: Excepted are the devoted servants of God.
versets.commentaire: Only the servants devoted to God are excepted. They alone describe Him as befits Him, for ikhlāṣ has purified their gaze; the sincere utters of God only what the light unveils to him.

versets.trad: As for you and what you worship,
versets.commentaire: You and your objects of worship. Neither the idolater nor his idol has hold over the heart God guards; seduction reaches only one who lends himself to it.

versets.trad: you cannot lead anyone into rebellion against Him
versets.commentaire: You cannot seduce anyone against Him. Temptation has power only in the measure permitted; none strays but he whose heart has already consented to straying.

versets.trad: save those destined to burn in the Blaze.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, against extreme determinism: you will seduce of men only the one whom I have let stray by My decree; through him they stray, not through your seduction. Temptation does not create the fall; it reveals an inclination already there.

mots.racine: ص ل ي / ج ح م

versets.ar: وَمَا مِنَّا إِلَّا لَهُ مَقَامٌ مَّعْلُومٌ
versets.tr: wa-mā minnā illā lahu maqāmun maʿlūmun
versets.trad: “There is none of us but has a known station, [say the angels].
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds three stations: the angels have a known station they do not cross or overstep; the friends of God have a veiled station, secret between them and God, that none penetrates; the prophets have a manifest station, upheld by dazzling miracles, for they are the models for creatures — the prophets' affair is in clarity, the friends' under the veil.

mots.racine: ق و م / ع ل م

versets.trad: We are indeed those ranged in ranks,
versets.commentaire: It is we, the ranged in ranks. The rank of the angels figures the perfect order of worship; each station keeps its place without encroaching, in the harmony of service.

versets.trad: and it is we who glorify God.”
versets.commentaire: It is we, the glorifiers. Rank and glorification go together: the order of service is the support of tasbīḥ. To keep one's place in humble worship is already to celebrate the transcendence.

versets.trad: The disbelievers used to say:
versets.commentaire: This is what the deniers used to say. Before the Reminder's coming, they claimed they would believe if they had it; but the heart that seeks excuses will not believe even before the light.

versets.ar: لَوْ أَنَّ عِندَنَا ذِكْرًا مِّنَ الْأَوَّلِينَ
versets.tr: law anna ʿindanā dhikran mina l-awwalīna
versets.trad: “Had we a Reminder from the ancients,
themes: the false desire for the Reminder
versets.commentaire: If only we had a Scripture of the ancients. They demanded a reminder like that of past nations; but the feigned desire for guidance does not engage the heart that will not surrender.

versets.trad: we would have been the devoted servants of God!”
versets.commentaire: We would be, they said, servants devoted to God. They laid claim to ikhlāṣ before ever testing it; but sincerity is not proclaimed in advance, it is proven in the trial of the response.

versets.trad: Yet they rejected it, and they will come to know!
versets.commentaire: Yet when the Reminder came, they rejected it: they will soon know. The claim collapses at the test of reality; those who swore to believe rejected, and the veil will fall when it is too late.

versets.trad: Our word has already gone forth to Our servants, the messengers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Our word preceded them with felicity, Our decree settled upon them with holy protection and care; they are, on Our part, helped. The victory of the messengers is inscribed from all eternity in the divine word.

versets.trad: They are the ones who will be helped, and
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whom He helps is not vanquished, and whom He subdues does not triumph. Divine help is an unshakeable guarantee; no force prevails against one whom God upholds.

versets.trad: Our host will bring them victory.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Our host are those He has raised to spread His religion and establish the Truth; whoever would humiliate them falls upon his face and is dragged in the rope of his own ruin. The soldiers of God are invincible because their strength is His.

versets.trad: So turn away from them for a while;
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: turn away from them, O Muḥammad, until their terms run out and their states come to an end; await the close of their days, for their affair will soon be undone. The prophet's patience rests on the certainty of the outcome.

versets.trad: and watch them, for one day they will see.
versets.commentaire: Watch them: soon they will see. What they deny today will be shown them tomorrow; the present blindness is but a reprieve before the compelled vision.

versets.trad: Do they seek to hasten Our chastisement?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they said this in demanding that the Hour come, pressing for it out of excess of ignorance, then out of lack of faith. To hasten the chastisement one denies is the height of heedlessness.

versets.ar: فَإِذَا نَزَلَ بِسَاحَتِهِمْ فَسَاءَ صَبَاحُ الْمُنذَرِينَ
versets.tr: fa-idhā nazala bi-sāḥatihim fa-sāʾa ṣabāḥu l-mundharīna
versets.trad: When it descends upon their courtyard, evil will be the morning of those who were warned!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the chastisement descends into their court and the trial settles at their threshold, their morning is dire; turn away from them, for soon will come what they were guarding against. The morning of the warned who did not listen is the darkest.

mots.racine: ص ب ح / ن ذ ر
mots.def: the dawn of those put on guard.

versets.trad: And turn away from them for a while, and
versets.commentaire: Turn away yet a while. The repetition of the command strengthens the patience; the prophet withdraws not from weariness but from confidence in the appointed term.

versets.trad: watch, for one day they will see...
versets.commentaire: And watch: soon they will see. The double repetition seals the certainty: the veil will fall, and what they refuse to see will impose itself on their eyes.

versets.ar: سُبْحَانَ رَبِّكَ رَبِّ الْعِزَّةِ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ
versets.tr: subḥāna rabbika rabbi l-ʿizzati ʿammā yaṣifūna
versets.trad: Glory be to your Lord, the Lord of Might, far above what they ascribe,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “Glory be to your Lord” — in sanctification of Him. The sūra seals itself as it had opened: by the proclamation of oneness, washing the Name of every unworthy ascription. The Lord of Might (ʿizza) infinitely transcends the descriptions of men.

mots.racine: ر ب ب / ع ز ز

versets.trad: and peace be upon the messengers,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: and peace upon Our prophets. The sūra that has told over the messengers gathers them in a common greeting; peace upon the messengers is the crowning of their faithfulness.

versets.trad: and praise be to God, the Lord of the worlds!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī seals it magnificently: “and praise be to God” — that is: He is the Praised, in evil as in good, in what harms as in what profits. Perfect praise embraces all states; the accomplished heart gives thanks in every circumstance.

mots.racine: ر ب ب / ع ل م
